description_long: This is a large commercial jet airliner with a wide fuselage, featuring a distinctive hump on the upper forward section, characteristic of the Boeing 747 family. The aircraft has four turbofan engines mounted under its low, swept-back wings, which are equipped with subtle winglets at the tips. The fuselage is primarily white with colored sections in green, red, blue, and purple, and features multiple rows of passenger windows along both sides. The tail assembly consists of a single vertical stabilizer and a conventional horizontal stabilizer. The landing gear is not visible in the images, suggesting it may be retracted. The cockpit is located at the very front of the fuselage, above the nose, with a multi-window configuration.
description: A large, multi-engine commercial jet airliner with a wide fuselage, swept low wings, and a prominent upper deck hump. The exterior is white with colored accents and features four underwing turbofan engines.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wide nose, cockpit windows, and the full span of the swept wings with four visible engines.
description_view_1: Left side profile highlighting the upper deck hump, rows of windows, and the underwing engines.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail assembly, horizontal stabilizers, and the full wingspan with engine placement.
description_view_3: Right side profile similar to the left, showing the colored fuselage, windows, and engine configuration.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing, swept-back wings with subtle upward winglets at the tips and underwing engine mounts.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers
attribute_landing_gear_type: Retractable tricycle landing gear (not visible in images)
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed multi-seat cockpit with multiple windows above the nose
